# Manifest type generation

> Epic: [Contract packages](../../epics/00-contract-packages.md)
> Theme: [Pillar finale](../../README.md) (federation)

## Purpose

Every pillar publishes a single, navigable TypeScript entry point that names its
whole public surface: the entities it owns, the errors it throws, and the router
it serves. That entry point is the `<Pillar>Contract` interface, generated into a
committed `manifest.generated.ts` and re-exported from the pillar's stable
`./manifest` package path.

Two distinct artifacts carry the word "manifest" in this codebase; keep them
apart:

| Artifact           | What it is                                                                                     | Where it lives                                    | Audience                                                |
| ------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
| `<Pillar>Contract` | A **compile-time** TypeScript interface — the structural snapshot of the pillar's public types | `src/contract/manifest.generated.ts` (committed)  | Cross-pillar consumers + the `@pops/pillar-sdk` typings |
| `ManifestPayload`  | The **runtime** JSON wire shape a pillar POSTs to the `registry` on boot                       | Built in `src/api/manifest.ts`; schema in the SDK | The `registry` pillar (validation + snapshot)           |

This PRD owns the **first** artifact (the generated type) and its codegen
pipeline. The runtime payload's _schema_ is owned by the SDK; this PRD documents
the payload only insofar as it is the other half of the "manifest" story and
shares the `./manifest`-adjacent surface. Registration mechanics belong to the
SDK/registry epics.

## Data model

### Generated file: `src/contract/manifest.generated.ts`

Committed to git and drift-checked in CI. One file per pillar contract.
Regenerated by the pillar's `generate:manifest` script (`pnpm -F @pops/<pillar>
generate:manifest`, or `tsx scripts/generate-manifest.ts` from within the
pillar). Shape (food shown):

```ts
// AUTOGENERATED by scripts/generate-manifest.ts. Do not edit.
// Regenerated by the pillar's `generate:manifest` script.
// Source version pinned at generation time: 0.1.0
import type { Ingredient } from './types/ingredient.js';
import type { MealPlan } from './types/meal-plan.js';
import type { Recipe } from './types/recipe.js';
import type { FoodError } from './errors.js';
import type { FoodRouter } from './router.js';

export interface FoodContract {
  readonly pillar: 'food';
  readonly version: string;
  readonly entities: {
    readonly ingredient: Ingredient;
    readonly mealPlan: MealPlan;
    readonly recipe: Recipe;
  };
  readonly errors: FoodError;
  readonly router: FoodRouter;
}
```

The interface has four members:

| Member     | Source                                                 | Notes                                                          |
| ---------- | ------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `pillar`   | string literal of the pillar id                        | e.g. `'food'`, `'finance'`, `'registry'`                       |
| `version`  | `string` (the value pinned in the header comment)      | The literal comes from `package.json`; the type stays `string` |
| `entities` | one `import type` per file under `src/contract/types/` | keyed by lowerCamel entity name                                |
| `errors`   | the pillar's `<Pillar>Error` union from `errors.ts`    | single re-export, not a per-variant map                        |
| `router`   | the `<Pillar>Router` type from `router.ts`             | the ts-rest contract router type                               |

### Stable re-export: `src/contract/manifest.ts`

Hand-written. Re-exports the generated type so the package path
`@pops/<pillar>/manifest` is stable even when the generator output
moves:

```ts
export type { FoodContract } from './manifest.generated.js';
```

This file also carries the pillar's `ModuleManifest` value (`id`, `name`,
`version`, `surfaces`, `settings`, …) consumed by `libs/module-registry`. That
value is unrelated to the codegen and is maintained by hand.

### Source files the codegen reads

```
pillars/<pillar>/src/contract/
├── types/<entity>.ts   # one type export per entity
├── errors.ts           # exports the <Pillar>Error union
├── router.ts           # exports the <Pillar>Router ts-rest contract type
└── ../../package.json  # `version` field (pinned into the header comment)
```

The generator imports each named symbol so a rename or deletion makes codegen
fail loudly rather than emit a broken manifest. The entity/error/router set is
declared in the renderer, not discovered by walking the AST (see Business
rules).

### Runtime counterpart: `ManifestPayload`

The JSON a pillar registers with the `registry` is **not** generated. It is a
hand-written value, built per pillar in `src/api/manifest.ts` and validated
against `ManifestPayloadSchema` (Zod) exported from
`@pops/pillar-sdk/manifest-schema`:

```ts
export function buildFoodManifest(version: string): ManifestPayload {
  return {
    pillar: 'food',
    version,
    contract: { package: '@pops/food', version, tag: `contract-food@v${version}` },
    routes: { queries: [], mutations: [], subscriptions: [] },
    search: { adapters: [] },
    ai: { tools: [] },
    uri: { types: [] },
    consumedSettings: { keys: [] },
    nav: FOOD_NAV,
    pages: [...FOOD_PAGES],
    healthcheck: { path: '/health' },
  };
}
```

`ManifestPayloadSchema` is the single source of truth for the wire shape across
the federation: `pillar`, `version`, `contract` (`package`/`version`/`tag`),
`routes`, `search`, `ai`, optional `sinks`, `uri`, `consumedSettings`, optional
`settings`/`nav`/`pages`/`assetsBaseUrl`/`captureOverlay`/`features`, and
`healthcheck`. It enforces identifier conventions (kebab pillar ids, camelCase
tool/adapter names, dotted procedure paths, `contract-<pillar>@v<semver>` tags)
at validation time.

## Tooling surface

### Per-contract scripts (`package.json`)

```jsonc
{
  "scripts": {
    "generate:manifest": "tsx scripts/generate-manifest.ts",
    "verify:manifest": "tsx scripts/verify-manifest.ts",
    "build": "tsx scripts/verify-manifest.ts && tsc -b tsconfig.build.json && tsx scripts/generate-openapi.ts && tsx scripts/generate-api-types.ts",
  },
}
```

`verify:manifest` runs **first** in `build`, so a stale committed manifest fails
the build before any compilation work happens.

### Scripts (live in each pillar's `scripts/`)

| Script                 | Role                                                                                                                |
| ---------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `generate-manifest.ts` | Reads the contract version, renders the interface, writes `manifest.generated.ts`, then normalises it with `oxfmt`. |
| `verify-manifest.ts`   | Re-renders + oxfmts in memory, byte-compares against the committed file, exits non-zero on drift.                   |
| `render-manifest.ts`   | Shared module: `readContractVersion()`, `renderManifest(version)`, `MANIFEST_OUTPUT_PATH`, `MANIFEST_HEADER`.       |

The generator's top-level `import type` of every entity/error/router symbol is
the load-bearing safety net: codegen will not compile if a named export
disappears.

### Package export

```jsonc
{
  "exports": {
    "./manifest": {
      "types": "./dist/contract/manifest.d.ts",
      "default": "./dist/contract/manifest.js",
    },
  },
}
```

## Rules

- **The generated file is committed and drift-checked.** Treated like the
  api-extractor snapshot — reviewed in the diff, enforced in CI.
- **Drift fails the build.** `verify:manifest` re-renders, oxfmts, and
  byte-compares; on mismatch it exits non-zero with
  "Run `pnpm -F @pops/<pillar> generate:manifest` and commit the result."
- **Output is normalised by `oxfmt`.** Both `generate` and `verify` pipe through
  `oxfmt --write`, so the committed file always matches workspace formatting and
  the byte-compare is stable. This is what makes the codegen idempotent in
  practice (re-running with no source change yields a byte-identical file).
- **The renderer is hand-maintained string templating, not an AST walk.**
  `render-manifest.ts` emits a fixed interface shape; the entity/error/router
  list is hardcoded in the renderer per pillar. The `import type` statements in
  `generate-manifest.ts` validate that those symbols still exist. There is no
  `ts.createProgram` extraction today.
- **`version` is `string`, with the concrete value pinned in the header
  comment.** The interface field is typed `string`; the generation-time version
  is recorded in `// Source version pinned at generation time: <v>` for review
  and drift signal.
- **The wire payload (`ManifestPayload`) is hand-written and Zod-validated.**
  Each pillar's `build<Pillar>Manifest(version)` constructs the value; the SDK's
  `ManifestPayloadSchema` validates it. There is no generated `ManifestPayload`
  type and no generic `buildManifestPayload()` helper — each pillar owns its
  builder.

## Edge cases

| Case                                                               | Behaviour                                                                                                       |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| A contract entity/error/router export is renamed or removed        | `generate-manifest.ts`'s top-level `import type` fails to resolve; codegen errors out instead of emitting junk. |
| `manifest.generated.ts` is hand-edited                             | `verify:manifest` detects the byte diff and fails the build with the regenerate instruction.                    |
| `manifest.generated.ts` is missing entirely                        | `verify:manifest` reports the file is missing and exits non-zero with the regenerate instruction.               |
| `package.json` has no string `version`                             | `readContractVersion()` throws "package.json must declare a string `version` field".                            |
| Formatting differs from `oxfmt` output                             | Drift check fails — both sides are oxfmt-normalised, so any divergence is real.                                 |
| Pillar owns no domain DB (orchestrator)                            | It registers a `ManifestPayload` with empty `routes`/`search`/`ai`/`uri`; it has no `<Pillar>Contract` codegen. |
| Wire payload pins a non-existent or malformed contract package/tag | `ManifestPayloadSchema` rejects it at validation time (regex on `CONTRACT_PACKAGE` / `CONTRACT_TAG`).           |

## Acceptance criteria

Generated type pipeline. The full codegen pipeline (generator + verifier +
build-time drift check) is live on **food, lists, cerebrum, inventory**.
**finance, media, registry** ship a committed `manifest.generated.ts` but do not
yet carry the `scripts/` or the `generate:manifest`/`verify:manifest` wiring, so
their generated file is currently unenforced — wiring them up is the remaining
work.

- [x] Each contract on the live set emits `src/contract/manifest.generated.ts`
      with a `<Pillar>Contract` interface (`pillar`, `version`, `entities`,
      `errors`, `router`) — food, lists, cerebrum, inventory. finance, media, and
      registry have the committed file but not the pipeline (see note above).
- [x] `generate-manifest.ts` renders the interface, writes the file, and
      normalises it with `oxfmt` (food, lists, cerebrum, inventory).
- [x] The generator imports every entity/error/router symbol so a missing export
      fails codegen loudly.
- [x] `render-manifest.ts` exposes `readContractVersion()`, `renderManifest()`,
      `MANIFEST_OUTPUT_PATH`, and the header constant; both generate and verify
      share it.
- [x] `verify-manifest.ts` re-renders + oxfmts in memory and byte-compares
      against the committed file, exiting non-zero on drift, on a missing file, or on
      a missing `version`.
- [x] `generate:manifest` and `verify:manifest` exist as package scripts; `build`
      runs `verify:manifest` before compilation (food, lists, cerebrum, inventory).
- [ ] finance, media, and registry carry the `scripts/` codegen and the
      `generate:manifest`/`verify:manifest` build wiring so their committed manifest
      is regenerable and drift-checked. (registry's contract additionally still uses
      legacy `Core*` type names — `CoreContract`/`CoreError`/`CoreRouter` — to be
      renamed to `Registry*` when wired up.)
- [x] `src/contract/manifest.ts` re-exports the generated type so
      `@pops/<pillar>/manifest` is a stable import path, and the package
      declares the `./manifest` export.

Runtime wire payload (built, schema owned by the SDK):

- [x] `ManifestPayloadSchema` (Zod) in `@pops/pillar-sdk/manifest-schema` defines
      the wire shape and enforces id/tag/path conventions.
- [x] Each pillar builds its `ManifestPayload` via a hand-written
      `build<Pillar>Manifest(version)` (in `src/api/manifest.ts` for the data
      pillars; registry uses `src/api/registry-manifest.ts` and orchestrator uses
      `src/manifest.ts`).

## Out of scope

- Registry storage + serving of payloads — Epic 02.
- The SDK's boot-time registration flow (POST to `registry`) — Epic 01.
- The richer "auto-emit schemas/search/ai/uri/settings into the typed contract
  via TS Compiler API" design — see [ideas/manifest-type-generation.md](../../../../ideas/manifest-type-generation.md).
- Manifest versioning beyond the contract's semver. The manifest is the
  contract's structural snapshot; same version as the contract.
- A wire format other than JSON.

```

```
